title: Join FastF1 race-state signals to create undercut/traffic and neutralization features
summary: FastF1 exposes track-status, race-control messages, weather, pit in/out timing, pit-stop counts, and stream gaps to leader/car ahead.
rationale: FastF1 exposes track-status, race-control messages, weather, pit in/out timing, pit-stop counts, and stream gaps to leader/car ahead. Those are exactly the ingredients behind real F1 pit calls: SC/VSC windows, traffic release, and undercut viability.
implementation_hint: For overlapping seasons/races, align rows to FastF1 session time and add features like SC/VSC active or just-ended, laps since yellow/VSC start, GapToLeader, IntervalToPositionAhead, prior pit-stop count, pit-lane in/out timing proxies, and weather deltas. Also derive ‘cheap stop window’ and ‘rejoin in traffic’ indicators.
expected_effect: High upside if the competition split includes the same public races, because current features lack explicit neutralization and traffic context.
risk: Timestamp alignment can be messy; if race naming or timing alignment is off, this adds noise fast. Availability may differ by season/session.
